qs.t: Differentiate between TCP/IP and OSI model.
Define Network. List a function of each layer of OSI reference model and compare it with TCP/IP model.
Explain client/server and P2P network model with their advantages and disadvantages. Discuss layers of TCP/IP model with suitable diagram.
Differentiate between Client Server and Peer to Peer architecture. Discuss the functions of each layer of OSI model.
Why do we need layered architecture in computer network? Discuss the function of each layer of TCP/IP networking model.
What is a protocol? List out the common protocols used at each layer of TCP/IP model. Differentiate between client-server and P2P network.
Compare OSI reference model and TCP/IP reference model mentioning their similarities and differences.
What do you mean by network architecture? Compare TCP/IP and OSI reference models. Explain X.25 Network with its key feature.
Why layering is important? Explain design issues for layers in detail. Mention service primitives for implementing connection-oriented service.

Compare OSI layer with TCP/IP Layer. Explain in which level of OSI layer following tasks are done: error detection, encryption, logical identification, socket connection, dialogue control, physical identification.
What are the features of Client/Server Architecture? What are headers and trailers and how do they get added and removed?
Why do we need layered architecture in networks? Differentiate between TCP/IP and OSI model.
Explain line of sight (LOS) propagation modes. Draw block diagram generic optical fiber (OF) communication system and its RF range.
List out the most common guided and unguided transmission media used in computer networks. Explain any one of the guided transmission media with examples.
What is transmission medium? Explain different transmission medium with their merits and demerits.
What is multiplexing? What is its importance in communication? Explain different types of multiplexing techniques.
What are the importance of switching and multiplexing? Explain different techniques of multiplexing with typical applications of each.
What is switching and multiplexing? Explain switching technique used in modern computer networks.
What do you mean by switching in communication? Compare switching with multiplexing. Explain the El Telephone hierarchy system.
What is switching? What are the various switching techniques? Elaborate packet switching with a proper diagram.

Define Throughput. A network with bandwidth of 20 Mbps can pass only an average of 18,000 frames per minute with each frame carrying an average of 20,000 bits. Calculate the throughput. Differentiate between Packet switching and Virtual Circuit switching.
What are the factors to be considered while selecting media for communication? Differentiate between datagram and virtual circuit switching approach with respect to Frame Relay Network.
Define transmission media. Compare among Twisted Pair, Coaxial cable and Fiber optic.
What is ISDN? Explain the working principle of ISDN with its interface and functional group.
What are the factors that cause packet delay in computer networks? What are the differences between circuit switching and packet switching?

title: Data Link Layer
sub: Error control · CSMA · Ethernet · WLAN · VLAN · ARQ

qs.t: Write different ways to correct backward error correction. Compare pure Aloha and slotted Aloha mentioning the condition for no collision.
What is CSMA/CD? Why is it not applicable in wireless LAN? What are the techniques used to avoid the possible collisions in WLAN? Explain.
How CSMA/CD works? Describe Ethernet (IEEE 802.3) frame structure with function of each field.
Explain the working principle of CSMA/CD with appropriate figure.
What is collision? How is it occurred? How the possibility of collision is reduced in IEEE 802.3 and IEEE 802.11? Explain.
What is collision? What are the problems due to collision? How the possibilities of collision be reduced in IEEE 802.3 and IEEE 802.11 protocols?
Explain Go-back-N ARQ and Selective Repeat ARQ with example. How CSMA/CD is better than CSMA?
What are the functions of data link layer? How to detect signal collision in CSMA/CD? List the ethernet cable specification standards for 802.3.

What is Hamming distance? How do you apply it in data link layer error control mechanism? Calculate the CRC for an 8 bit sequence 11001101. Generator polynomial is x^4+x^2+1. Also find the transmitted bit frame.

What is piggy-backing? How do you apply it in data link layer flow control mechanism? Calculate the CRC for a 10 bit sequence 1010001101. Generator polynomial is x^5+x^4+x^2+1. Also find the transmitted bit frame.
Explain how CRC detects errors. M(x)=x^7+x^4+x^3+x^2+1, generator G(x)=x^3+1. Show actual bit string transmitted; if third bit is inverted, show how error is detected.
What is the main functionality of data link layer? Differentiate between circuit switching and packet switching.

State the various design issues for the data link layer. What is piggybacking? A bit string 0111101111101111110 needs to be transmitted at data link layer. What is the string actually transmitted after bit stuffing?
What is a virtual LAN? Why is it called as virtual? Explain how frames can be delivered from one computer to another within the same VLAN across different switches.
Briefly explain different types of Data Link Layer framing mechanisms. List the features of FDDI.
What do you understand by Media Access Control? What is its significance in data link layer? Explain why token bus is also called as the token ring.
Write short note on: VLAN.

title: Network Layer
sub: IP addressing · Routing · IPv6 · NAT · ARP · ICMP · Congestion

h: Distance vector vs link state routing

qs.t: What are routing protocols? Explain OSPF process in link state routing.
Subnetting: Company XYZ, IP 160.24.96.0/21, 6 departments (1024, 750, 254, 500, 151, 45 users) + point-to-point links. List CIDR, network address, broadcast address, usable host range and wasted IP.
Subnetting: IP 202.97.43.0/25, 4 LANs with 14, 55, 10, 29 hosts. Find subnet mask, network address, broadcast, usable host IP ranges.
Subnetting: Block 200.74.20.0/24 for 5 departments (4, 54, 120, 12, 30 hosts). List network address, broadcast, usable host range and wasted IP.
Subnetting: Block 16.16.16.0/21, divide equally for 5 departments. List network address, broadcast, subnet mask and usable IP.
Subnetting: Block 10.20.30.0/24, 4 LANs (4, 64, 24, 18 hosts) + 4 WAN links. List subnet wasted IPs.
Subnetting: Block 200.80.40.0/24 with 5 departments (29, 5, 16, 43, 14 hosts) + point-to-point links. List subnet mask, network address, broadcast, usable host IP ranges and wasted IPs.
Subnetting: ISP block 172.24.96.0/21 for 4 departments A, B, C, D (750, 200, 500, 45 hosts) + 3 point-to-point links. Find network address, broadcast, subnet mask, usable host range.
Subnetting: Block 202.50.0.0/24, 6 departments (125, 59, 27, 14, 4, 2 hosts). Find subnet mask, network address, broadcast, wasted IPs and usable host ranges.

Subnetting: Company leased 222.70.94.0/24 for 5 departments (50, 30, 25, 12, 10 hosts) + 2 point-to-point links. List network address, broadcast, usable IP range, subnet mask; also mention unused IP range.
Subnetting: Block 192.168.1.0/24 for 6 departments (16, 32, 61, 8, 6, 24 computers). Find network address, broadcast, usable IP range, subnet mask.
Subnetting: Block 120.89.96.0/19, 4 departments A, B, C, D requesting 100, 500, 800, 400 addresses. Perform subnetting with minimum wastage.
Subnetting: Pool of Class C, allocate 32, 65, 10, 21, 9 IPs to departments A–E with minimum loss.
Subnetting: Block 202.77.19.0/24, allocate 30, 24, 25, 20 IPs to 4 departments with minimum wastage.
Subnetting: Block 10.10.10.0/24, 4 departments (5, 16, 23, 27 hosts). Find subnet mask, network address, broadcast, unassigned range.
Subnetting: Block 202.70.64.0/24, 4 departments (A=23, B=18, C=28, D=13 hosts). Find subnet mask, network address, broadcast, usable host range.
Subnetting: Block 202.70.64.0/19, 4 departments requesting 100, 500, 800, 400 addresses.
Subnetting: Block 200.10.80.32/25, 5 departments requiring 5, 40, 28, 12, 6 hosts.
What is adaptive and non-adaptive routing? List properties of link state routing and mention how Designated Router (DR) is elected in OSPF routing.
Define routed and routing protocol. Explain RIP routing operation with its timer details.
What is unicast and multicast? Compare distance vector routing protocol and link state routing protocol with examples.
What is DR and BDR in OSPF? How do OSPF routers come into fully adjacency states?
Define routing algorithm. List properties/goals of routing algorithm. What is link state routing? Show how routing tables are populated in LSR with example.
Why routing is essential in computer networking? Compare distance vector routing algorithm with link state routing algorithm.
What is routing? Differentiate between distance vector and link state routing algorithms.
List IPv6 extension headers in order. Explain ISATAP and 6-to-4 tunneling with their address format for IPv4 to IPv6 transition.
Map IPv4 addresses with its IPv6 equivalent. What are the latest best IPv6 transition methodologies? Explain anyone.
Critically compare IPv4 and IPv6 in terms of routing and head manipulation. Explain the importance and implementation of 6RD for IPv6 based services on existing IPv4 networking.
Compare the IPv4 header with IPv6 header. Explain the dual stack strategy to transit from IPv4 to IPv6.
List the advantages of IPv6 over IPv4. Explain any two suitable transition strategies for IPv4 to IPv6.
What are the problems of IPv4? How can IPv6 reduce these problems? Explain header translation mechanism for transition from IPv4 to IPv6.
Explain three address types in IPv6 with IP notations. How does an IPv6 machine acquire IPv6 address automatically?
What is ICMP? Explain the importance and uses of ICMP in TCP/IP protocol suite.
What is the purpose of TTL and protocol field in IPv4 header? Which protocol provides feedback to hosts/routers? What is ARP and how does it work?
Why TCP is known as reliable protocol? What are the congestion control techniques? Discuss Token Bucket approach and compare it with leaky bucket.
How does Token Bucket control the congestion over the Leaky Bucket algorithm?
What are the services provided by Transport layer? Explain about Leaky-Bucket algorithm for congestion control.

title: Transport Layer
sub: TCP · UDP · 3-way handshake · Flow control · Sockets

l: UDP use cases despite being unreliable

qs.t: Write UDP header field and functions. Explain TCP 3-way handshaking for connection establishment and release.
Discuss UDP header and compare it with TCP. What is port address? Explain briefly about leaky-bucket algorithm used for traffic shaping.
Though UDP is said to be unreliable protocol, it is used in Internet. Why? Explain the three way handshake principle of a TCP connection between client and server.
What are the services provided by the transport layer? Explain connection establishment and connection release of TCP for data transfer with suitable diagrams.
What are the features of UDP protocol? In which case is UDP preferred as a transport layer protocol? Discuss with practical examples.
Explain the TCP segment structure. Why TCP is known as reliable protocol and also describe how reliability is provided by TCP.
What are the differences between TCP and UDP services? Explain the TCP datagram format in detail.

Why do you think that there exist two protocols in transport layer whereas there exists only one protocol in Internet layer in TCP/IP reference model? Explain token bucket algorithm for congestion control.
Why port number is used in networking? What are the services of transport layer? Differentiate between TCP and UDP protocol.
What is port number? Why is it necessary to standardize the port numbers for well-known servers? What happens when a web service is hosted at some different port such as 8765 instead of 80?
How connection is established and released in TCP. Explain Token Bucket algorithm.
Explain the TCP protocol with its Header. What do you understand by socket? Explain with its importance.

title: Upper Layers & Network Design
sub: DNS · HTTP · FTP · SMTP · POP · IMAP · DHCP · Proxy

l: Web server vs file server communication

qs.t: Compare DNS recursive query vs. iterative query. Explain iterative query for browsing www.youtube.com.
How does a DNS recursive query work? Discuss DHCP lease renew process with example diagram.
What do you mean by DNS delegation? List the step-by-step working principle of SMTP.
What is DNS? Why is it used? How is the DNS request from a client computer resolved from the authoritative server? Explain with necessary diagrams.
What is DNS server? Explain the recursive and iterative query.
What is the importance of DNS? How can a DNS request from a client computer be resolved from authoritative server?
What is DNS? Explain the working principle of DNS with a proper diagram. Compare IMAP and POP3 protocols.
What is a proxy server? Why is it used? Discuss briefly on HTTP and HTTPS services.
What is HTTP protocol? With an example explain how a request initiated by a HTTP client is served by a HTTP server.
What is recursive and iterative query? Explain with suitable diagram. Discuss the DNS records.
What are resource records in DNS? Explain the types of DNS queries with example.
How does an FTP client connect to an FTP server? Compare POP3 and IMAP protocols.
SMTP is a text based protocol and uses 7 bit ASCII. How can this be used to transmit images? Explain.
Which protocols are used in sending and receiving an email? Illustrate with necessary figure. Give a comparison of POP3 and IMAP.
What is TFTP? Explain working principle of FTP with data transfer process including proper port connection.

title: Advanced Topics
sub: Network security · Cryptography · RSA · Firewall · SDN · VPN

qs.t: What do you mean by firewall? Encrypt and decrypt the word "attack" using RSA.
What is router ACL? How do you apply ACL to block the IP network 202.70.91.0/24 incoming to interface Fast Ethernet of a router? Encrypt the word "ISPNet" using any suitable AES technique.
What are the fundamental differences between AES and DES? Encrypt the word "ComNet" using any suitable AES technique.
What is public key cryptography? Encrypt the word "security" using the RSA algorithm. Also show the decryption to obtain the plaintext.
What is a digital signature? Encrypt the message "PANDEMIC" using RSA algorithm. Also obtain the plaintext from the ciphertext.
Write down the steps involved in RSA encryption algorithm. Encrypt the word "Computer" using RSA algorithm.
How does a Digital Signature work? Encrypt the word HELLO using RSA algorithm. Also decrypt it by showing steps.
What is PGP? Use RSA algorithm to encrypt/decrypt the word COW.
List properties of secure communication. Encrypt and decrypt "ROSE" using RSA algorithm.
Explain the desirable properties of secure communication. Explain how packet filtering firewall works.
Compare symmetric key encryption method with asymmetric key encryption. Explain RSA algorithm with example.
What is network security? How can firewalls enhance network security? Explain how firewalls can protect a system.
Explain briefly how firewalls protect network and explain different types of firewall.
What is SDN? Explain its architecture with necessary diagrams.
What is VPN? Encrypt a message "network" using RSA algorithm.
Write short note on: AH and ESP (IPSec).
Write short note on: Digital Signature.
What is firewall? What are their types? Encrypt and decrypt "OVEL" message using RSA algorithm.
